constraints: Architect all session collections protocol messages UI and save ownership for up to four players
Validate the first networked loop with two players before three-player and four-player graduation
Do not treat two-player success as proof of four-player readability bandwidth or balance
Keep co-op post-MVP relative to the offline Windows vertical slice
Use relay-assisted player-hosted sessions rather than mandatory dedicated servers
Target responsive enjoyable cross-region play rather than promising literal zero latency
Use local response smoothing anticipation reconciliation buffering and packet-loss handling where needed
Simulate latency jitter packet loss and asymmetric links during validation
Test severe divergent enemy and attack states explicitly
Preserve a later host migration or transferable authority path
Resolve the local player's movement firing incoming damage pickups loot and immediate inventory feedback locally
Prioritize feel over strict centralized moment-to-moment authority
Accept weaker anti-cheat guarantees for the initial friendly cooperative model
Defer exact shared team-objective simulation beyond the first co-op MVP
Retain a minimal canonical layer for navigation completion teleports rewards and durable campaign facts
Use local combat simulation with shared confirmation for room identity doors completion map access and major rewards
Allow players to join a teammate's active room instance
Allow players to enter their own local version of an available room
Lock active-player-count scaling per room instance at engagement start
Permit exact enemy combat and pickup states to diverge locally while preventing permanent route and campaign disagreement
Defer strict bullet-level agreement advanced instance reconciliation and deep shared objectives
Decide next how global room completion doors and rewards settle across parallel instances

open_questions_added: Global room completion and door-unlock semantics across joined and separate local instances
Reward and banking settlement when party members clear different instances
Behavior when one instance clears while another remains in combat
Reconnect and host-migration handling for local room instances
Unity relay and networking package selection
Exact client trust validation and anti-cheat boundary
Cross-region latency jitter and packet-loss acceptance thresholds
Future shared-objective synchronization beyond the first co-op MVP

next_action.instruction: Ask exactly one concise A/B/C decision card selecting how durable room completion, doors, and rewards settle when players may either join a teammate's active room instance or enter their own local instance: the first valid instance clear marks the room globally complete; every participating active player's local instance must clear before global completion; or a hybrid where personal instances and ordinary loot resolve separately while coarse shared map progress unlocks after one valid clear and players still fighting may finish or withdraw. Preserve local authority for movement damage pickups loot and immediate feedback, coarse shared confirmation for room identity doors completion and major rewards, optional joined or personal room entry, personal lives and spectator clutch play, active-player-count scaling locked per instance at entry, separate personal progression, and deferred deep shared team objectives. Include pacing, griefing, split-party deadlock, farming, banking and unsecured loot, respawn and teleport behavior, disconnected or spectating players, synchronization complexity, and future deeper co-op objectives. Put the recommendation after all A/B/C options at the end of the card. D-191 through D-194 were persisted early for handoff safety; queue D-195 through D-200 and persist at D-200 unless the user changes the rule or requests another handoff.
